text.title: confidence-thresholded-cascade Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure: build eval, run cheap-only, bucket confidence, set threshold, deploy with telemetry, monitor.

step.name: build-eval-set
step.input: Production samples or representative synthetic tasks (>=100).
step.action: Collect inputs + ground-truth answers; balance across the difficulty spectrum.

step.name: run-cheap-only
step.input: Eval set + cheap model + Pydantic schema with reasoning/answer/confidence/requires_escalation.
step.action: Run every eval input through the cheap model; record answer, confidence, and the IDK flag.

step.name: bucket-and-measure
step.action: Bucket predictions by confidence (0.0-0.5, 0.5-0.7, 0.7-0.85, 0.85-0.95, 0.95-1.0). Compute accuracy per bucket.
step.output: Calibration table: bucket → accuracy → support count.
step.decision-gate: If accuracy in the top bucket is below the target SLA, the cheap model is the wrong choice; swap models and restart at step 2.

step.name: set-threshold
step.action: Pick the lowest bucket whose accuracy meets the target SLA. The threshold is that bucket's lower bound.

step.name: deploy-with-telemetry
step.action: Wire the cascade per `templates/two-level-cascade-pydantic.py`. Add the spans from `templates/span-instrumentation.py`. Ship behind a feature flag.
step.output: Production traffic on cascade, with per-request spans capturing model used and confidence.

step.name: monitor-and-recalibrate
step.input: Rolling escalation-rate metric, sampled production accuracy.
step.action: Alert if escalation rate moves more than 2x from baseline. Recalibrate quarterly or after any prompt or model change.
step.decision-gate: If escalation rate spikes >2x, freeze the cascade and rerun steps 2-4 with the current model snapshot.
